text:
Market today: Middle East tensions are pushing up Treasury yields and oil prices, creating headwinds for growth-oriented tech and chip stocks. Citi's call to retire the "Magnificent Seven" narrative suggests a potential shift away from broad tech leadership.

Your holdings: QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and QQQI are all in "SELL" status and showing losses, with SOXL down a painful -46.77%. SPAL, a new leveraged ETF, is also down significantly. Remember, margin amplifies these drawdowns.

Watch: Keep a close eye on oil prices and Treasury yields; their direction will heavily influence your tech-heavy book. Also, monitor if the "Magnificent Seven" truly lose their market leadership.

Bottom line: Focus on managing your "SELL" positions and protecting capital, given the macro headwinds and leverage.
plays: Review SOXL: Your stop at 200 is far above the current price of 135.47. Re-evaluate this stop immediately to protect capital from further decay in this leveraged ETF.
Consider SOXS: Given the "SELL" status on your semi holdings and rising yields, the inverse semi ETF SOXS (WATCH 8/12) could be a short-term hedge. Scanner entry 52.77-52.27, stop 51.07.
Tighten QQQ/SMH stops: With QQQ at -2.7% and SMH at -12.55% and both on "SELL," consider tightening stops to protect against further downside in these core positions.
